question: A company tracks its average website sign-ups per year. Based on the chart, which of the following statements is most appropriate?

options.A: The latest year has clearly had the fewest sign-ups of the entire period.
options.B: The latest year shows a sharp decline in sign-ups compared to previous years.
options.C: From this chart, no firm conclusion can be drawn about the full-year trend in the latest year.
options.D: Sign-ups have remained almost unchanged in every year shown.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 4 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (422 sign-ups) directly to full-year values like 1,108 sign-ups creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
